mission.title: kubevirt: Source VM remains in Running after live migration, resulting in two instances running
mission.description: Source VM remains in Running after live migration, resulting in two instances running. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kubevirt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubevirt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
In a 1.6.0 environment, after performing a VM live migration, the source VM does not shut down properly upon completion, causing two instances to run simultaneously.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Source VM remains in Running after live migration,…
steps.description:
Delivery of signals can actually drop signal
if multiple signals are generated while the signal is being blocked.
In this case only one signal is delivered after it is un-blocked. Because we always do Wait4 per signal we can actually miss a process being terminated.
Sometimes the ordering happen to
```yaml
ps aux
USER         PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
qemu           1  0.0  0.0 1679844 13328 ?       Ssl  Aug21   0:00 /usr/bin/virt-launcher-monitor --qemu-timeout 248s --name rhel9-3195 --uid 0db4e8a0-c0e4-4e95-9b1c-bd7f2a16c8d4 --namespace vm-ns-32 --kubevirt-
qemu           8  0.0  0.0      0     0 ?        Z    Aug21  13:51 [virt-launcher] <defunct>
qemu         444  0.5  0.0   4452  2688 pts/0    Ss   09:19   0:00 bash
qemu         445  0.0  0.0   7032  2688 pts/0    R+   09:19   0:00 ps aux
```

steps.title: Confirm Source VM remains in Running after live… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Delivery of signals can actually drop signal
if multiple signals are generated while the signal is being blocked.
In this case only one signal is delivered after it is un-blocked. Because we always do Wait4 per signal we can actually miss a process being terminated.
Sometimes the ordering happen to be unfortunate and the virt-launcher process is missed and never cleaned up.
codeSnippets:
ps aux
USER         PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
qemu           1  0.0  0.0 1679844 13328 ?       Ssl  Aug21   0:00 /usr/bin/virt-launcher-monitor --qemu-timeout 248s --name rhel9-3195 --uid 0db4e8a0-c0e4-4e95-9b1c-bd7f2a16c8d4 --namespace vm-ns-32 --kubevirt-
qemu           8  0.0  0.0      0     0 ?        Z    Aug21  13:51 [virt-launcher] <defunct>
qemu         444  0.5  0.0   4452  2688 pts/0    Ss   09:19   0:00 bash
qemu         445  0.0  0.0   7032  2688 pts/0    R+   09:19   0:00 ps aux

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
